children.name: Pluvianus
children.description: A monotypic genus of striking wader once placed in the pratincole family Glareolidae but now recognised as a distinct evolutionary lineage. The Egyptian Plover is famous for its supposed symbiotic relationship with crocodiles, cleaning parasites from their mouths — a behaviour documented by Herodotus but rarely observed in modern times.

children.name: Pluvianus aegyptius
children.description: A boldly patterned black, white and grey bird of sandy riversides across sub-Saharan Africa. It has a distinctive breeding strategy, burying its eggs in warm sand and regulating incubation temperature by wetting its belly feathers with water to cool them. The species is extirpated from Egypt despite its common name.
